name: baseline-passes
notes: Hand-built smoke fixture (8 files: lib/main.dart, lib/src/auth/state.dart, lib/src/network/client.dart, lib/src/network/response.dart, lib/src/services/service.dart, lib/src/util/loggable.dart, lib/src/util/string_ext.dart plus vendor/ignored.dart and generated/widget.g.dart) parsed cleanly by package:analyzer 7.7.1 on Dart 3.12.1. Oracle reports 21 TP / 0 FP / 0 FN over Class/Trait(Mixin)/Method/Field/Function declarations after applying the dart-codegen glob (`**/*.g.dart`, `**/*.freezed.dart`, `**/*.mocks.dart`) symmetrically; meets gate floor P>=0.93 R>=0.85 from gates.rs.
Baseline build_phases=crawl:1ms parse:1ms decl_graph:0ms full_graph:0ms total:4ms; oracle_ms=5145 (analyzer cold start). No oracle-level FP/FN to repair.

Part-file members are emitted under their declaring file path on both sides for now (mirrors the current squeezy tree-sitter behavior). When the graph resolver lands `__dart_part_of__` re-parenting per spec §4a, both the oracle helper (dart_oracle.dart) and the squeezy-graph dart resolver will switch to emitting part members under the host library's file — bench update should land in the same PR to keep the (file, kind, name) keys aligned.
